title: Why did the angels all attacked one by one and never in a group
display_title: Why did the angels all attacked one by one and never in a group
wikitext:
[]: Why did the angels all attacked one by one and never in a group?             Quote Post by TraxXavier » Thu Dec 31, 2015 12:40 pm Why did the angels all attacked one by one and never in a group? I'm quite puzzled by that, it does not seam a reasonable tactic. A strange point is the falling (kamikaze) angel, as its behavior in the way stated in the show would not benefit him but only those that would came after him. Thats a clear sign of an attempt on cooperation, self sacrifice even. But if so than they should be able also to just attack in a group. Also the tactic of the fallen angel is rather strange, I mean why not blowing up when being caught by unit 01, would units 01 AT field made that much of a difference to the effect? If the angel is such a great bomb that shouldn't make a difference. Or for that mater detonating shortly before unit 01 deployed its At field. An other thought I head about this angel what if his objective was not to blow up but rather just break through all the armor covering the geo front directly into terminal dogma. Or possibly a variation of that, something like a explosively formed penetrator, with the intention to shoot its core directly into Lilith strait through the surface. Imho with an AT field it could protect a piece of it self from the effect of the explosion and arrive at the target mostly unscathed. In rebuild we see that in the large butterfly like corpus of the angel there was a smaller angel body located inside on the front. So like it wanted to detonate and shoot this central mass into terminal dogma. Given that speculations the angels actions appear not longer selfless but in fact just as individualistic and targeted on merging with Lilith as of all the others. Hence we are back to the question why all of the angels acted individually and did not want to cooperate with the others. A possible strait forward explanation would be that whatever angel would reach Lilith would get to shape the new world as he sees fit, hence all angels would not only fight against NERV but would also be competing with each other. An other nice example of competition between individuals ruining a collectively achievable chance of a great success.

[]: Re: Why did the angels all attacked one by one and never in a group?             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Dec 31, 2015 12:51 pm The little we do see of Angel psychology gives the impression that, Tabris aside, the idea of communicating with another being comes as quite the novelty to them. Coordinating their attacks would seem out of character (as well as not complying with the "monster of the week" format).

[]: Re: Why did the angels all attacked one by one and never in a group?             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Dec 31, 2015 2:52 pm Because they aren't a unified group. They want different things and may not even be aware each other exists. Basically you're framing this wrong. it's not humans vs. angels, it's a bunch of angels (one of which is humanity) all on their own. The only reason they keep attacking humanity rather than each other is because we start the game with possession of both the seeds which is what most of them seem to want.

[]: Re: Why did the angels all attacked one by one and never in a group?             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Dec 31, 2015 2:54 pm TraxXavier wrote: Why did the angels all attacked one by one and never in a group? I'm quite puzzled by that, it does not seam a reasonable tactic.

[]: Re: Why did the angels all attacked one by one and never in a group?             Quote Post by FullMetalBiscuit » Thu Dec 31, 2015 6:00 pm The Angels are all born from Adam, but they are not one single species such as Humans. They are, "humans who cast aside human form" essentially meaning that each Angel is a different species in itself, while remaining genetically similar to humans. And since they are all different, they do not attack together. I believe that I'm right there anyway, going by memory.

[]: Re: Why did the angels all attacked one by one and never in a group?             Quote Post by xPearse » Thu Dec 31, 2015 6:27 pm Because they're a plot device.

[]: Re: Why did the angels all attacked one by one and never in a group?             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Thu Dec 31, 2015 6:40 pm I doubt it's that simple

[]: Re: Why did the angels all attacked one by one and never in a group?             Quote Post by xPearse » Fri Jan 01, 2016 7:01 pm Arcadia's legacy wrote: I doubt it's that simple

[]: Re: Why did the angels all attacked one by one and never in a group?             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Fri Jan 01, 2016 7:06 pm And what of the explanations the others above have given?

[]: Re: Why did the angels all attacked one by one and never in a group?             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Jan 02, 2016 3:56 am xPearse wrote: Because they're a plot device.

[]: Re: Why did the angels all attacked one by one and never in a group?             Quote Post by TraxXavier » Sat Jan 02, 2016 4:39 am Arcadia's legacy wrote: I doubt it's that simple

[]: Re: Why did the angels all attacked one by one and never in a group?             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Jan 02, 2016 5:20 am TraxXavier wrote: thats a nonexplanation.

[]: Re: Why did the angels all attacked one by one and never in a group?             Quote Post by Compiling_Autumn » Sat Jan 02, 2016 2:54 pm In Ep.24, Kaworu more or less explains the desire of the Angels to merge with Adam as a kind of loneliness. Having the Angels communicate with each other would have changed all that. My personal headcanon for Sahaquiel (the 'kamikaze Angel') is that he was unaware that slamming into the Earth would probably destroy him. He just kept moving towards Adam, not thinking about how solid the ground would be. The thing he always wanted was never really available to him, because of how he adapted.

[]: Re: Why did the angels all attacked one by one and never in a group?             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jan 04, 2016 3:41 am It's a bit difficult to address the OP in a meaningful way, considering no two Angels (except Israfel, if that's actually a two-in-one, which isn't possible to rule out) ever even encounter each other in life. The in-universe reason for this seems to be that their incubation periods are "stacked" in a way that's convenient for Monster of the Week script-writing: cf. Sandalphon, who according to Ritsuko hatched prematurely at a time when several of its siblings had already completed their development, gone to Tokyo-3, and been killed.

[]: Re: Why did the angels all attacked one by one and never in a group?             Quote Post by TraxXavier » Mon Jan 04, 2016 4:41 am Reichu wrote: The in-universe reason for this seems to be that their incubation periods are "stacked" in a way that's convenient for Monster of the Week script-writing:

[]: Re: Why did the angels all attacked one by one and never in a group?             Quote Post by Chuckman » Tue Jan 05, 2016 11:40 pm Seele controls the Angels and was releasing them according to a schedule to make sure the prophecy unfolds and the proper mythopoeic energies are directed towards their goal. It is known.

[]: Re: Why did the angels all attacked one by one and never in a group?             Quote Post by TraxXavier » Wed Jan 06, 2016 7:42 am Chuckman wrote: Seele controls the Angels and was releasing them according to a schedule to make sure the prophecy unfolds and the proper mythopoeic energies are directed towards their goal. It is known.

[]: Re: Why did the angels all attacked one by one and never in a group?             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Jan 06, 2016 9:39 am Chuck's version of Eva rarely matches up with the actual product, but I often wish it did.

[]: Re: Why did the angels all attacked one by one and never in a group?             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Wed Jan 06, 2016 9:48 am We all wish it did.

[]: Re: Why did the angels all attacked one by one and never in a group?             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Jan 06, 2016 2:37 pm TraxXavier wrote: I think that is not likely, as if it would be so they would wait with the angel releasing until the EVA series would be ready and than they could kill all the angels without any risk.
